title: Cheque Imaging
body_text:
Cheque Imaging
1	Introduction to Cheque Imaging
1.1	Overview of Cheque Imaging
Central 1 uses cheque imaging to capture Images of credit union deposit items, Chargebacks, and Incoming Clearing Items drawn on the credit union. The basic cheque imaging service allows credit union staff to view and print Images of Incoming Clearing Items and cheques on deposit via the Online Return System (ORS).
Central 1 makes Image Files available to the credit union or its statement service provider if the credit union wants to have Cheque Image Statements available to its members. Image Files are an optional service.
Changes to the
Bills of Exchange Act
and CPA Rule A10, Images, Image Printouts and Return Replacement Documents, extend the same legal standing to cheque Images as to physical cleared cheques. As such, it is unnecessary to return physical cleared cheques, including lawyersГўВҖВҷ and realtorsГўВҖВҷ trust cheques, to credit unions or the credit unionГўВҖВҷs members. Unqualified Items and Chargebacks are the only physical items that are sent to the credit union. Physical items are typically destroyed at Central 1 after 14 days. However, some credit unions have legacy arrangements in place which allow for shipment of the physical items to a storage facility.
Central 1 uses cheque Images to enter dishonoured items into the clearing system on the credit unionsГўВҖВҷ behalf. First, the credit union must select the cheque(s) that it wants to dishonour in ORS. Central 1 then determines the branch of first deposit, prints a Return Replacement Document (RRD) or Image Printout of the dishonoured item, and returns the cheque through the Clearing system.

1.2	Cheque Imaging Services Available at Central 1
Cheque Image Files for Statements
Central 1 provides files of cheque Images for use with member statements. Credit unions or their service provider can use the Image Files to include Images of Incoming Clearing Items in their membersГўВҖВҷ statements. This is an optional service.
Image Access for Members on Internet Banking
Members can access Images of Incoming Clearing Items via Internet banking applications for up to seven years after the processing date. In order to offer this service, the credit unionГўВҖВҷs Internet banking provider must have an interface that supports Central 1ГўВҖВҷs cheque imaging system. The length of time the Images are available online is dependent upon the Internet banking provider.
